So what we like to do is focus on what we call the three pillars of fitness success. You know, you go to weight watchers and you get the nutrition and the accountability, but no exercise. Right. It's the exercise that helps build the muscle, keep a strong endurance. Your bone density increases, stuff like that. You go to a big gym like the ones we have around here and yeah, you get a membership for super cheap and then they hope you don't come. Yeah. They call you here. There's not much guidance. Or something. Yeah. Unless you want to spend thousands on personal training and stuff. And so we wanted to find that happy medium as a business as a facility. And so what we like to focus on number one is exercise, obviously. And as you can see, we're well equipped to get you guys in shape. And so what that means here is three days a week. I'm going to end three days a week during the challenge and you're working in a group setting with other people. Everybody here since this is our grand opening of our second location, every member is going to be doing the challenge. And so everyone's working towards that goal together, like Larry said. And so, you know, just that dynamic alone has an added accountability rate to it. There's going to be two coaches on staff that are making sure everything's safe and effective. If there's things you can't do, we can modify. So you we really want to take care of everybody and encourage do the encouragement route less than the drill sergeant route. Right. So number two is nutrition. And that's what a lot of us are missing, especially in America. You know, we're either bombarded with fast food or a bombarded with fad diets. And so what we like to do is eliminate the fad diet part. Depending on your weigh in, we will we will customize your meal plan to what you need. And it's all whole food. We also include a 42 recipe cookbook so you know what you're doing with it and to make it delicious. So 42 days, 42 recipes. So you have a recipe for every day. What else we got? We got restaurant guides that are included in that. So, you know, we live life. So when you go out to eat, you'll be able to refer to the restaurant guide so you don't sabotage what you've been working so hard for. We also have meal prep packs, which what that means is we teach you how to get your meals prepared on like a Sunday and 90 minutes or less for the week because the worst thing you can be is hungry and unprepared. Because that's when we grab the fastest thing, which is more than likely the worst thing we can eat. So that kind of covers the nutrition. And then the third component is what a lot of places are missing and that's the accountability and it binds the other two together. You can have all the info you want on nutrition and exercise, but if you don't do it or no one's holding you to it, you're not going to get the results. So we have a three point system aside from just the group fitness and being accountable to the trainer that's, you know, waiting for you every day. We provide weekly phone calls. Actually, we go use a like a texting app. It's like a walkie talkie app. And so that's how we contact you. But every week we get ahold of you ask how you're doing. Are you staying motivated? How are you doing this week? You know, and any concerns or questions or if you're struggling, it's a great time to get questions answered and just kind of use them as a sound board like your own psychologist, you know, your own fitness psychologist. I should say. And number two, we have weekly weigh ins. And so it's not like we want to focus on just one number, but we need to know that we're making progress every week because it's all about progress for not making progress. We lose momentum. We lose motivation. I imagine that's what's happened with some of you that have tried it. It's like you go for a couple weeks and you don't see any changes. And it's like, why am I bothering doing this? So we have that added accountability. And then the final one, everybody's put into the same Facebook group. And so it's, we try to build a positive community for everybody and people feed off each other's energy and share recipes and stuff like that. So it's a really cool dynamic and most of your materials, at least the printed materials, you can access through that. And so, and then the final one, no, that is. For group workouts and stuff, do you guys have flexible times? Oh, yeah. Yes. So we offer six times in the morning from 5.15 to 9.45. And then five times in the evening from 4.30pm to 8.15pm. So we try to make it as accessible as possible. Every 45 minutes, if you come in at 5.15, you'll be walking out at six guaranteed every day. Yeah. But only Monday through Friday? Monday through Friday. And then we have Saturday from 8.15 every 45 minutes for four classes. And then closed on Sundays because people are at church, stuff like that. And so that's the vehicle or food prepping. Sundays are for chopping veggies. And so as far as the challenge goes, there's four stipulations or four rules to stay accountable to. And number one, our whole goal is to get you to lose 25 pounds or 6% body fat. We understand not everybody has to lose 25 pounds or should they lose 25 pounds? Like you. And so what we do is we take your BMI at your weigh-in. And if it's above a certain level, it's safe to lose 25 pounds. And that's what we push for. If it's below a certain level, we go for the 6% body fat. Because for some people, a 5-pound fat loss could be 6% of your total body weight. So we want to make that safe and effective. We don't want to force anybody into starving or anything like that. And so, and then number two is the three workouts a week with a check-in on Facebook. And what that does for us, it's another level of accountability. This program is all about accountability because we want you guys to succeed from start to finish. And we've put $1,000 worth of materials for you guys to actually get there. And so, where were they? I think you kind of put two together because you did the workouts. Oh yeah. And so that's kind of the concept of the challenge. I mean, can you put yourself there in six weeks, 25 pounds lighter with some new clothes? No, I can see that. Yeah. So what's happened in the past? We've run these in the past and absolutely free. We get 100, 150 people sign up. And then come Monday morning. What do you think happens? They don't show. 20, 30% actually show up. And even more disheartening for us was at the end of the six weeks is two to five percent actually complete the challenge. And so it got mentally exhausting because we did get into this to help people. And when you can't help somebody and I can't go home in your pocket, I wish I could, but you couldn't afford me. No, I'm just kidding. Slap food out of your hand or something. It just, you know, it gets to be really frustrating. So what we did is we went back to the drawing board and we're like, how can we hold people accountable but also make it effectively free? And so what we've done is after we instituted this little trick, I would say, is accountability rose by 87%. So out of the hundred, we had 87% complete the challenge. And out of that, up to 94% had success in the challenge. And so what was cool for us as a business at the end of the six weeks, 80% of the people that completed the challenge fell in love with us and stayed on and just continued their training. And so what that is, is basically you're making a bet against yourself. And so it is a deposit. And at the end of the six weeks, as long as we reach 25 pounds or 6%, you've hit your every workout. We've made the way in so we get the review. That deposit is 100% refunded to you and you can be done, right? The review and say thank you or no, thank you, do whatever you want. But we also wanted to reward people as well as taking that and putting it towards future training, right? And so that deposit is 299. It doesn't all have to be due today. We would just need a deposit today and then it would be three payments. So October 6th and October 20th. So as long as it's paid by October 20th. That has just, like I said, it just skyrocketed the results and the accountability because once people have that skin in the game, it becomes real. You know, it's not just, oh, I signed up, but I went halfway. I'll do the next challenge. We wanted to make a lifestyle change for people.
